cover.title: THE SUN
cover.subtitle: The one who keeps the lights on, even when they're running low.
cover.tagline: You brighten the room first. You're still learning that you don't have to perform to stay loved.

profile_scores.caption: High Expression and Pride, Low Stability. You reach out first and set the mood, while your own lower moments can stay out of sight.

short_summary: You change the mood the moment you enter a room, but people often notice your energy before they notice you.
The loneliness isn't from being unpopular. It's from being known mostly through the version of you that's always on.
What you're learning is that the right person stays connected even when your light is lower.

full_report.headline: THE SUN
full_report.intro: You make people laugh, lift the mood, and bring warmth into the room. This report follows what can sit underneath that light: why you keep performing even when you're tired, who enjoys your energy without really knowing you, and what starts to change when someone stays close after the lights go down.

chapters.title: When You Love
body: You're the kind of person who changes the mood the moment you walk into a room.
Even an awkward gathering loosens up once you're in it. You speak first, laugh first, set the mood first.
People often take that for granted. It comes so naturally that no one stops to notice it.
You're the same way in love. You make them laugh. You keep things moving. You hear "it's fun being with you" a lot.
But under all that brightness, there's another question.
"Do they still want me around when I'm not the fun one?"
That's the part people miss.
You can look completely fine while checking, somewhere inside, whether the connection is still there.
chapters.profile_note: High Expression and Pride, Low Stability. You reach out first and set the mood, while your own lower moments can stay out of sight.

chapters.title: The Pattern You Keep Repeating: THE EMPTY SPOTLIGHT CURVE
body: At the start of a relationship, you're usually the brightest person in the room.
Being together is fun. The mood is easy. The conversation never seems to run dry.
People get drawn to that fast.
Then the relationship gets deeper, and you start doing more to keep that feeling alive.
When something's hard, you laugh it off. When the mood dips, you lift it again.
And little by little, the real you behind that energy shows up less.
People know your spark. Fewer people know your tired days, your uncertainty, or the moments when you don't know what to say.
That's where the loneliness starts.
chapters.highlight: You're not lonely because you're unpopular. You're lonely because people know the version of you that's always on.

chart.title: THE EMPTY SPOTLIGHT CURVE
chart.description: As the relationship deepens, your effort to keep the connection bright can grow while less of your unguarded self shows.

chapters.title: What You've Gotten Wrong About Yourself

items.belief: I'm just naturally the mood maker.
items.truth: You really do have that gift. The problem starts when you feel responsible for keeping every room, date, and conversation alive. A still moment doesn't mean the connection is gone.
items.truth_short: Your energy is real. It doesn't have to carry the whole relationship.

items.belief: People like me because I'm fun.
items.truth: Your energy may be part of what draws people in. But it isn't all of you. A deeper relationship also has room for your serious days, your uncertainty, and the moments when you don't feel like performing.
items.truth_short: Being fun can start the connection. Being fully known is what deepens it.

items.belief: If I show a weak side, people will leave.
items.truth: That fear makes sense when you're usually the one holding the mood together. The other person may not respond perfectly the first time. What matters is whether they stay curious, come back, and make room for the version of you that isn't shining.
items.truth_short: You don't need a perfect reaction. You need room to be real.

chapters.title: The Person You Keep Choosing
body: You're especially drawn to people who respond strongly to you.
They laugh hard at your stories. They notice you in a crowded room. They make you feel chosen.
That kind of attention can feel electric.
And the better they react, the more you want to shine.
The problem starts when their interest is strongest only while you're entertaining, exciting, or easy to enjoy.
At some point, you need to know whether they like the energy you create or the person carrying it.
chapters.highlight: Someone can love your spark without knowing how to care for you when it fades.

chapters.title: What Actually Changes Something
body: You don't need to become less bright. That energy is part of who you are.
What you need is one person who gets to see what's backstage too.
Not everything. Not all at once.
Just one real thing.
That today was rough. That something still bothers you. That you're tired and don't want to lift the mood right now.
This doesn't ruin the connection.
It gives the other person a chance to know you beyond the performance.
chapters.action_sentence: It gives the other person a chance to know you beyond the performance.

chapters.title: Where You Go From Here
body: You already know how to light up a room. You already know how to draw people in. You already know how to lift the mood.
The next part asks something else of you.
How to trust that a low energy day won't end the connection.
How to let someone stay close when you're tired, serious, or unsure.
How to stop turning every hard moment into something easier for everyone else.
You don't have to dim yourself. You're learning that love can stay even when you're not performing.
chapters.transition_note: You know how to bring light into a relationship. Now you're learning that connection can stay when your light is lower.

chapters.title: The Types You Keep Falling For

items.type_title: The Person Who Reacts To Everything
items.description: They laugh loudly. They get excited. They make you feel interesting. You shine even brighter around them. The problem starts when their reaction gets smaller and you start working to get it back.

items.type_title: The Person Who Makes You Feel Chosen
items.description: Even in a crowded room, their attention lands on you. They remember what you said. They notice small things. That can feel deeply reassuring. Just make sure the attention grows into real curiosity about your inner life.

items.type_title: The Person Who Relies on You for the Mood
items.description: They love the warmth you bring, but they rarely create it themselves. Over time, you can feel less like a partner and more like the person responsible for keeping the relationship alive.

items.type_title: The Person Who Loved the Beginning
items.description: At first, they react to everything. Then the excitement settles. You try harder. You get brighter. But the real question is whether they know how to build closeness after the newness wears off.

chapters.title: The Warning Signs You've Missed
chapters.intro_note: These signs can show up early. You just tend to brighten the mood before they fully register.

signal_strength.label: You're almost always the one setting the mood, while they only react
When something hard comes up, you laugh it off first so the mood doesn't sink
You laugh more in front of people, then your energy suddenly drains when you're alone
They barely know about the moments you were actually struggling

chapters.core_signal: They know how you make the room feel. They may not know how you feel inside it.

chapters.title: What a Healthy Relationship Looks Like for You

checklist.title: They like you without the performance.
checklist.description: On low energy days, serious days, and ordinary days, the connection still feels steady.

checklist.title: They stay curious about your inner life.
checklist.description: They don't only react to your energy. They ask what was hard, what stayed with you, and what you need.

checklist.title: Warmth moves both ways.
checklist.description: You're not always the one making plans, fixing distance, or bringing the mood back.

checklist.title: You don't have to recover on command.
checklist.description: You can be tired or unsure without being pushed back into the version of you they prefer.

chapters.title: What Happens In Your Next Relationship

sections.who_breaks_you: A hard match for you is someone who enjoys your brightness but brings very little once the excitement settles. They react while you perform, then pull back when you need steadiness instead of applause.
sections.who_steadies_you: A steadier partner stays engaged when your energy changes. They ask, initiate, and help create connection without waiting for you to do all the emotional work.
sections.same_pattern_repeats: You may still feel drawn to people who respond strongly to your presence or make you feel especially chosen. That doesn't decide the ending. What matters is whether the relationship keeps growing when you're no longer creating every spark.
sections.next_signs: The signs may show up in small moments. You stop filling a pause. You say you're tired. You let the mood stay ordinary. One response tells you very little. What keeps happening afterward tells you more.
sections.try_this: Tell one trusted person something real without turning it into a joke. Say the day was hard, that you need reassurance, or that you don't have the energy to lift the mood. Say it plainly, without using the moment to measure them. After that, pay attention to what they keep doing. Do they stay close? Do they ask more? Do they bring care back toward you?
sections.final_line: What matters isn't a single response. It's whether the relationship keeps making room for every side of you.

action_plan.summary: You don't need to dim your light. You need a relationship that stays connected when you're not the one creating it.
action_items: Tell one trusted person when your day was hard without turning it into a joke.
Let one ordinary pause stay as it is instead of lifting the mood right away.
Watch whether the other person starts creating connection and care too.

closing.message: You've spent a long time learning how to set the mood. This report looked at what happens when that role becomes expected, and how intimacy grows when your lower moments are allowed to stay visible.
closing.cross_sell_cta: Curious what your partner's pattern looks like? See their Cosmic Blueprint.
